es-MX.title: Mira
es-MX.subtitle: Reproductor de medios personal
es-MX.description:
Mira es un reproductor multimedia personal que se conecta a tu propio servidor de medios usando tus credenciales. Reproduce tu biblioteca personal de películas y series.

Mira NO incluye, aloja ni distribuye ningún contenido ni catálogo. Es únicamente un reproductor: funciona igual que VLC o Infuse y requiere que tú aportes tu propio servidor de medios. No proporcionamos contenido, catálogos ni suscripciones de ningún tipo.

Características:
• Reproduce películas y series de tu propio servidor
• Continuar viendo y favoritos
• Selector de audio y subtítulos
• Búsqueda y filtrado por categorías
• Tus credenciales se guardan cifradas en el dispositivo y nunca se envían a nosotros

en-US.title: Mira
en-US.subtitle: Your personal media player
en-US.description:
Mira is a personal media player that connects to your own media server using your own credentials. It plays your personal library of movies and series.

Mira does NOT include, host, or distribute any content or catalog. It is only a player — like VLC or Infuse — and requires you to bring your own media server. We do not provide any content, catalogs, or subscriptions of any kind.

Features:
• Play movies and series from your own server
• Continue watching and favorites
• Audio and subtitle track selection
• Search and category filtering
• Your credentials are stored encrypted on the device and are never sent to us

review.notes:
GUIDELINE 5.2.3 — NO THIRD-PARTY CONTENT IS INCLUDED:
Mira is a generic media player client, directly comparable to VLC, Infuse, or a Plex/Jellyfin client. It does NOT include, host, distribute, aggregate, or provide ANY streaming content, channel list, catalog, or discovery service. Mira ships completely empty. Before any content appears, the user must connect their OWN media server using credentials from their own separate subscription. The app has no backend infrastructure and bundles no content of any kind.

We therefore cannot provide 'documentary evidence of rights' to third-party streams, because the app contains no third-party streams. Every piece of content an end user sees comes exclusively from a server that the user themselves owns or subscribes to — entirely outside our control and Apple's. This is the same architecture as every personal media-server client on the App Store.

The ONLY media bundled anywhere in the app is in the optional DEMO MODE, and it is exclusively royalty-free, openly licensed content that we have the right to use: Blender Foundation open movies under Creative Commons Attribution — Big Buck Bunny (CC BY 3.0), Elephants Dream (CC BY 2.5), Sintel (CC BY 3.0), Tears of Steel (CC BY 3.0), Cosmos Laundromat (CC BY 3.0), Caminandes (CC BY 3.0). License pages: https://peach.blender.org/about/ , https://orange.blender.org/ , https://www.sintel.org/about/ , https://mango.blender.org/about/ , https://gooseberry.blender.org/ , https://www.caminandes.com/ . The demo contains NO live TV channels and NO third-party catalog — only these CC-licensed films and short series.

Terms of Use (full text: https://kevinarce98.github.io/mira-tv-mobile/terms/, also linked on the setup screen and in Settings) require the user to confirm they are solely responsible for ensuring the content they access is lawfully licensed before any connection can be made. The setup screen requires checking 'I have read and accept the Terms of Use' before connecting.

HOW TO REVIEW (demo mode, no subscription needed):
1. On the setup screen, check 'I have read and accept the Terms of Use'.
2. Tap 'Explore in demo mode' (below the 'or' divider).
3. The catalog loads with the CC-licensed Blender movies and series. Tap any title to play — playback fully works. Browsing, search, favorites, continue watching, settings, theme, and language switching are all functional.
